questions: Were the three file changes implemented correctly following established patterns?
Do typecheck and build pass after all changes?

answers.answer: Yes. All three files were modified following the exact patterns from sibling commands: (1) artifacts.ts now imports getFlag, accepts args: string[], reads --run via getFlag, and conditionally passes runId via queryParams using object spread. (2) index.ts line 36 shows [--run <runId>] in usage text, and line 79 passes rest to cmdTicketsArtifacts. (3) artifact.ts wraps the hxFetch call in try-catch with a clean error message including step/repo context and process.exit(1).
evidence: src/tickets/artifacts.ts lines 3, 18-23 — getFlag import, args param, runId extraction, conditional queryParams
src/tickets/index.ts line 79 — rest forwarded to cmdTicketsArtifacts
src/tickets/artifact.ts lines 42-54 — try-catch around hxFetch with console.error and process.exit(1)

answers.answer: Yes. Both npm run typecheck (tsc --noEmit) and npm run build (tsc) pass cleanly with exit code 0 and no error output.
evidence: npm run typecheck — exited 0, no errors
npm run build — exited 0, no errors

statement: Implementation complete. Three files modified in helix-cli: artifacts.ts (--run flag support), index.ts (router forwarding and usage text), artifact.ts (404 error handling). Both typecheck and build pass. All changes follow established patterns from sibling commands.
